author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Shipped a 3-property MVP harness at `crates/jjf-storage/tests/proptest_harness.rs`
(landed in `28e5ed71`) targeting the public mutate/read surface of `jjf-storage`:

1. **`round_trip_create_then_read`** — every `IssueDraft` field that
   survives the storage stamp (title, body, slug, type, labels,
   assignee) comes back equal on `Storage::read`. Status-stamped
   fields (id, status, timestamps) are excluded. Labels compared as
   sorted/deduped (matches the writer's normalization).
2. **`status_machine_no_panic_matches_oracle`** — generated
   `(source_status, [verb])` sequences against one issue. For each
   verb, the oracle (`predict_post_status`, mirroring the b2 table in
   `experiments/qa-redteam-2026-06-25/sub2-wronganswer.sh`) predicts
   either a successful land-status or a typed `Error::Invalid`
   rejection. Failed mutations must leave the source status atomic.
   Panics fail the property. This is the property that would have
   falsified `121f48b` (silent-success on a closed/abandoned source).
3. **`read_after_write_idempotent`** — two consecutive `Storage::read`
   calls after any mutation return equal `Issue` records. Catches
   snapshot-cache invalidation regressions.

Plus a non-proptest `harness_bootstrap_smoke` sanity check. Each case
spins a fresh `Storage::init` v3 scratch repo (zero jj calls — just
the sentinel-ref plant) under `tests/.scratch/proptest-<pid>-<n>/`,
and pins the wall clock via `JJF_TEST_CLOCK_SECS` so timestamps don't
drift. 16 cases per property; ad-hoc
`PROPTEST_CASES=N cargo test --release` cranks coverage on demand
(stress-tested clean at 128 cases per property).

Wall-clock: ~11s release / ~28s debug for the whole harness file.
Total workspace nextest count went from 579 to 583 (3 property tests
+ 1 smoke).

No real bugs surfaced. This is the durable defense for future QA
rounds; the QA round 2026-06-25 already shipped fixes for the bugs
that retroactively justify the harness.

# Recommendation

Close. The MVP cut is in, properties hold under stress, and the
follow-ups for the deferred properties are listed below as concrete
next tickets.

# Confidence

High on what shipped: 64 cases × 3 properties × multi-verb sequences
exercises a large slice of the status machine, and the oracle is
derived directly from the b2 shell matrix that the QA round already
hardened. Medium-low confidence that this harness alone would catch
the NEXT regression class — the deferred properties (4 and 5) are
where I'd expect the next high-leverage finds to come from. Filing
them as follow-ups (below).

# Open follow-ups

- **Property 4 (list_ready monotone-with-closure)**: closing an
  issue can only ADD members to the next `list_ready` result;
  opening one can only REMOVE. Would have falsified the mixed-kind
  dep cycle `121f48b` directly. Punted for the time budget; needs a
  multi-issue generator with extant dep targets.
- **Property 5 (list_ids cardinality)**: after N successful
  `create_issue` calls on a fresh repo, `list_ids().len() == N`.
  Simple; punted only because it's lower-leverage than the others.
- **Multi-issue / dep-edge generator**: `IssueDraft.dependencies`
  is empty in the MVP because drafting deps requires extant target
  ids. A multi-stage generator that creates N issues then drafts
  edges among them would extend Property 1's coverage and enable
  Property 4.
- **Cycle-rejection property**: assert that `add_dep_edge` never
  lands an edge that would close a `Blocks`-or-`ParentChild` cycle
  — even in the presence of in-flight retries. Would have caught
  `43c7615` / `121f48b` cleanly.
- **Memory surface**: `Storage::set_memory` / `unset_memory` /
  `read_memory` aren't covered yet. Same shape as the issue
  round-trip, much smaller in scope.
